isolation.sharedState: Dashboard reads from threat-radar-mcp API at http://localhost:9001

VAL-PI-001.title: Connections Lane Renders with Fuchsia Accent
VAL-PI-001.evidence: DOM inspection confirms Π lane renders with class 'lane lane-pi', CSS custom property --lane-accent set to var(--fuchsia). Lane icon text is 'Π'. Accent color rgb(217, 70, 239) confirmed on 16 elements (lane icon, header, scores, tabs). Hue ≈292° falls within the required 290–320° fuchsia/magenta range.
VAL-PI-001.details: JavaScript eval extracted lane structure: lane-pi class present, Π symbol visible as lane icon, fuchsia accent rgb(217,70,239) applied consistently across bridge card scores, type badges, feedback loop SVG nodes, and tab controls.

steps.observed: Page title 'Threat Radar Wall', all 3 lanes visible (η, μ, Π)

steps.action: Extract Π lane icon color via JavaScript eval
Count fuchsia accent elements in Π lane
steps.observed: 16 elements with rgb(217,70,239) including icon, header, scores, tabs

VAL-PI-002.title: Bridge Card Scores
VAL-PI-002.evidence: DOM inspection via JavaScript eval confirms 2 bridge cards, each displaying 3 labeled numeric scores: Realism (100), Fear (84), Public Benefit (28 and 37). Scores rendered in pi-scores-row with pi-score-label and pi-score-value elements.
VAL-PI-002.details: Bridge card 0: Realism=100, Fear=84, PublicBenefit=28. Bridge card 1: Realism=100, Fear=84, PublicBenefit=37. Both cards have data-testid='pi-scores-row' with labeled score items.

steps.action: Extract bridge card score data via JavaScript eval
steps.expected: Each bridge card shows 3 labeled numeric scores
steps.observed: 2 bridge cards with Realism, Fear, Public Benefit scores displayed as labeled numbers

VAL-PI-003.title: Bridge Card Local Action
VAL-PI-003.evidence: Both bridge cards contain a 'Suggested Action' section (data-testid='pi-suggested-action') with actionable text: 'Monitor "Detailed / Analysis / Chokepoint / Affects" for developments affecting local context'. The action text references the global thread and directs local monitoring.
VAL-PI-003.details: Bridge card 0: Suggested Action present with text referencing global thread title. Bridge card 1: Same pattern. Both have pi-action-label 'Suggested Action' and pi-action-text with actionable content.

steps.action: Extract suggested action sections from bridge cards
steps.expected: Each bridge card contains a 'suggested action' section with actionable text
steps.observed: Both bridge cards have 'Suggested Action' label with text: 'Monitor "Detailed / Analysis / Chokepoint / Affects" for developments affecting local context'

VAL-PI-004.title: Federated Coordination Path
VAL-PI-004.evidence: Both bridge cards contain a 'Coordination Path' section (data-testid='pi-coord-path') with text: 'Coordinate information sharing through federated assessment exchange'. This describes multi-node cross-community coordination via federation.
VAL-PI-004.details: Bridge card 0: Coordination Path present with federated coordination text. Bridge card 1: Same pattern. Both reference 'federated assessment exchange' — a multi-node coordination mechanism.

steps.action: Extract coordination path sections from bridge cards
steps.expected: Each bridge card contains a coordination path with multi-node coordination text
steps.observed: Both bridge cards have 'Coordination Path' label with text referencing 'federated assessment exchange'

VAL-PI-005.title: Feedback Loop Visualization
VAL-PI-005.evidence: SVG diagram with aria-label 'P→R→N→Π→A feedback loop' renders 5 circle nodes (P=Perception, R=Reduction, N=Narrative, Π=Connection, A=Action) connected by 4 forward arrows and 1 dashed feedback arc from A back to P. Text label 'feedback' present at bottom of diagram.
VAL-PI-005.details: FeedbackLoopDiagram component renders SVG viewBox='0 0 300 80' with 5 labeled nodes, 4 inter-node arrows (line elements with markerEnd), and 1 curved feedback path (dashed arc). The 'feedback' text label appears at (160, 78).

steps.action: Extract intelligence loop SVG structure via JavaScript eval
steps.expected: 5 labeled nodes forming P→R→N→Π→A cycle with feedback arc
steps.observed: SVG contains 5 circle+text groups (P, R, N, Π, A), 4 forward arrows, 1 dashed feedback path, 'feedback' text label. aria-label='P→R→N→Π→A feedback loop'

VAL-FED-002.title: Federated Comparison Panel
VAL-FED-002.evidence: Clicking 'Federation View' button opens pi-federation-panel with 3 peer nodes displayed side-by-side: 'This Instance' (online, Realism=100, Fear=84, PublicBenefit=28, synced just now), 'Peer Alpha' (online, Realism=100, Fear=79, PublicBenefit=40, synced 5m ago), 'Peer Beta' (stale, Realism=90, Fear=99, PublicBenefit=21, synced 2h ago). Panel uses grid layout with 3 pi-federation-peer elements.
VAL-FED-002.details: Federation panel renders ≥2 columns (3 total) each labeled with peer node identifier, showing assessment values for shared thread. Grid layout confirmed via pi-federation-grid class. Each peer shows realism/fear/publicBenefit score bars with numeric values.

steps.action: Click 'Federation View' button on first bridge card
steps.expected: Federation comparison panel opens with ≥2 peer assessments
steps.observed: Panel opened showing 3 peers: 'This Instance' (online), 'Peer Alpha' (online), 'Peer Beta' (stale) with side-by-side score bars

steps.action: Extract federation panel structure via JavaScript eval
steps.observed: 3 pi-federation-peer elements in pi-federation-grid, each with realism/fear/publicBenefit scores and status badges

VAL-CROSS-004.title: Global Signal → Π Lane Action
VAL-CROSS-004.evidence: Bridge cards in Π lane reference global thread 'Detailed / Analysis / Chokepoint / Affects' (η tag) linked to local threads 'Training / Analysis / Electricity / Costs' and 'Cloud / Spot / Pricing / Dropped' (μ tag). These same local threads appear in the Action Feed (μ lane): 'Training / Analysis / Electricity / Costs' as coordination opportunity, 'Cloud / Spot / Pricing / Dropped' as this-week suggestion. Action Feed visible with 12 items across 3 time horizons.
VAL-CROSS-004.details: ConnectionOpportunity flow verified: (1) Bridge card 0 references global η thread 'Detailed / Analysis / Chokepoint / Affects' with scores Realism=100, Fear=84, PublicBenefit=28. (2) Local thread 'Training / Analysis / Electricity / Costs' from bridge card appears as Action Feed item 8 (coordination opportunity). (3) Bridge card 1 references same global thread → local 'Cloud / Spot / Pricing / Dropped', which appears as Action Feed item 5 (this week). Action Feed shows 12 suggestions grouped by time horizon: 2 within-24-hours, 5 this-week, 5 coordination-opportunity.

steps.action: Extract bridge card global thread references via JavaScript eval
steps.observed: 2 bridge cards, both linking global 'Detailed / Analysis / Chokepoint / Affects' (η) to local threads (μ)

steps.observed: 12 action feed items; 'Training / Analysis / Electricity / Costs' (coordination opportunity) and 'Cloud / Spot / Pricing / Dropped' (this week) both present — matching bridge card local thread references
